text.title: Internal Linking Graph Worked Example
text.summary: End-to-end worked example: 120-page solo content site with 6 clusters; audit finds 11 orphans, fixes applied, all clear after re-crawl.

section.title: Setup
section.p: Solo founder runs a content blog at blog.example.com. 120 indexed pages across 6 topic clusters: saas-pricing, stripe-ops, indie-launch, marketing-attribution, customer-research, productivity. First quarterly audit using this methodology.

list.item: Orphans: 11 pages (4 in productivity cluster, 3 in customer-research, 4 stale stragglers).
Low-inbound spokes: 17 pages with exactly 1 inbound (all from the hub).
Exact-match anchor offenders: 4 hub URLs averaging 18 inbound links each, all with the head-term anchor.
Missing hub backlinks: 9 spokes referencing hub only in a "see also" footer chrome.
Orphans (11): 7 folded into closest cluster with new sibling inbound; 4 de-indexed (stale).
Low-inbound spokes (17): added 1-2 sibling inbound links per spoke (anchor diversified).
Exact-match offenders: rewrote 8-12 anchors per target to use partial-match + descriptive forms.
Missing hub backlinks: moved the link into the spoke body (paragraph 2).

section.title: Re-crawl results
section.p: Outcome: hub for saas-pricing moved from position 14 to 8 over the next 60 days; spoke pages picked up 1.8x organic traffic month-over-month.

text.example: 120 pages + 6 clusters + 1 hub each + ≥4 spokes per hub + re-crawl shows all audit lists empty → APPROVE via run-the-checklist.
